meta.m_title: Connect any AI assistant to IQ Option · MCP Integration Guide
meta.m_desc: Connect Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor and 12+ AI assistants to your IQ Option account via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). One Bearer token, four market servers — digital options, CFD, crypto, forex. No code, three steps.
meta.m_og_title: Connect any AI assistant to IQ Option · MCP Integration Guide
meta.m_og_desc: One token, four markets, any AI. Connect Claude, ChatGPT or Cursor to IQ Option via MCP in three steps.
meta.m_tw_title: Connect any AI assistant to IQ Option via MCP
meta.m_tw_desc: One token, four markets, any AI. Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor — connected to IQ Option in three steps.
meta.m_article_headline: Connect any AI assistant to IQ Option via MCP
meta.m_article_desc: One Bearer token, four market MCP servers (digital options, CFD, crypto, forex). Ready-made configs for Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor and 12+ assistants. No code, three steps.
meta.m_howto_name: How to connect an AI assistant to IQ Option
meta.m_howto_desc: Connect any MCP-capable AI assistant to IQ Option in three steps.

strings.t0014: One token, four markets, any AI of your choice. Talk to Claude, ChatGPT or Cursor about your positions, markets and trades. No code, three steps.
strings.t0026: One protocol. Any AI talks to your account.
strings.t0027: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard from Anthropic through which AI assistants connect to external systems. We host servers for each instrument type — you create a token and paste it into your assistant.
strings.t0029: Ask AI about live quotes, historical candles, spreads, volumes — in your own words, across all 4 markets at once.
strings.t0031: Open positions, pending orders, trade history, total equity — all through plain conversation. No window switching.
strings.t0033: Each token is a separate integration with its own lifetime. See all active ones in Settings → AI integrations and revoke any of them with one click.
strings.t0036: Each instrument type lives on its own server. A single token from your IQ Option settings works on all four — connect all of them or just the ones you need.
strings.t0046: Create one Bearer token in IQ Option (
strings.t0048: ) and reuse it in all four configs. Below we provide a ready-made config that connects all markets in a single block.
strings.t0050: Read by default. Trade — opt-in, per market.
strings.t0051: Every token has its own scope. Read access to all 4 servers is enabled automatically and cannot be turned off — AI needs it just to "see" your data. The right to
strings.t0053: is granted explicitly — separately for each market, when you create the token.
strings.t0058: Balance, quotes, history, open positions, pending orders
strings.t0065: Long/short on CFD instruments with leverage
strings.t0071: EUR/USD, GBP/JPY and other currency pairs
strings.t0080: Without read access AI is useless — it can't show your balance or prices. That's why the read scope is enabled automatically across all 4 servers. This is the safest level: AI only looks, never touches.
strings.t0082: Want AI to help trade only options? Tick
strings.t0083: and nothing else. Margin CFD/Crypto/Forex stay read-only — even if AI tries, the server returns
strings.t0084: Scope can be changed without recreating the token
strings.t0085: Want to drop a trade permission from a token that's already in use? Open
strings.t0086: , find the token, uncheck the market — the permission is dropped instantly. The token value itself does not change, so no need to update your assistant's config.
strings.t0088: Pick the one closest to your use case
strings.t0090: AI helps analyse markets, read your portfolio, calculate P&L — but never trades. The safest setup.
strings.t0100: . Options, CFD and forex remain untouchable for AI.
strings.t0103: Read + Trade on all 4 servers. Maximum capability — and maximum responsibility. Only when you fully trust this specific assistant.
strings.t0106: Three steps. No code, no terminal.
strings.t0107: Our MCP runs over streamable-HTTP — no Python environments, no virtual venv, no repos to clone. Just a URL + Bearer token you create in one click.
strings.t0109: In the mobile app or on the web:
strings.t0114: Give the token a name and an expiry
strings.t0115: Tick which markets are allowed for trading (read is always on). Details — section 03 above
strings.t0116: Copy the token immediately — once you close the screen it won't be shown again
strings.t0119: Native MCP support is available in:

strings.t0133: Pick your assistant — the config is ready.
strings.t0134: Each snippet connects all 4 servers at once. Just replace
strings.t0136: with the token you created in step 1. If you only need one market, keep just that block and delete the rest.

strings.t0213: Before starting a conversation, click the Connectors icon under the prompt and select the ones you need. Available on Plus / Pro / Team subscriptions.

strings.t0232: Open the Agent panel in Zed — the servers should appear under Available Tools.

strings.t0241: Multi-model desktop AI client with native MCP. Fully via UI — no JSON required.
strings.t0244: In the chat pick a model → there's a Tools icon on the right → pick the servers for your session.

strings.t0254: Your own agent, in-house platform, or any system that speaks MCP per spec. Universal parameters:
strings.t0259: If curl returned JSON with a tools list — your token and URL are working. From there, implement the MCP client per
strings.t0262: Your token, your responsibility. Guardrails are built in.
strings.t0263: MCP gives AI the keys to your trading account. So we — and you — watch the limits together.
strings.t0264: Token lives in headers, not in chat
strings.t0265: The Bearer token is attached to the HTTP header on the assistant ↔ server connection. AI does not see it in the message body and cannot accidentally send it out.
strings.t0267: The token = access to your trading account. Never publish in git, screenshots, chats or gists. If you suspect a leak — open
strings.t0269: and hit Revoke next to that token. All requests using it stop working instantly.
strings.t0271: You set the expiry at creation. After that date the token stops working automatically. We recommend keeping separate tokens per integration — that way you can revoke only what you need.
strings.t0273: AI is powerful but not infallible. It can pick the wrong instrument, size, or direction. Before trusting the model with size — walk through scenarios manually and double-check its suggestions. The responsibility for every trade is yours.
strings.t0275: What to ask AI once connected.
strings.t0276: Plain language — no commands or slashes. Ask in English, Spanish, Portuguese, or your own language — the model figures out which server to call.
strings.t0279: Which account am I on right now
strings.t0281: How much free margin do I have
strings.t0283: Which assets are available on Margin Crypto
strings.t0285: Compare BTC movement over the last hour
strings.t0286: What are the current spreads on the top 5 forex pairs
strings.t0291: How many trades did I make today
strings.t0293: Open a long BTC at 0.01 with a 5% stop loss
strings.t0296: Place a limit buy on SPY around 580
strings.t0299: Do I need to install anything on my computer?
strings.t0300: No. Our MCP servers are hosted by us — no repos to clone, no Python or venv to install. Just the URL + Bearer token in your AI assistant's config.
strings.t0301: Does one token really work on all 4 servers?
strings.t0302: Yes. Create a single Bearer token in
strings.t0303: and use it in the Authorization header for all four endpoints: digital-options, marginal-cfd, marginal-crypto, marginal-forex.
strings.t0304: What if I only want to connect one market, e.g. Forex?
strings.t0305: Delete the other blocks from the config — keep only
strings.t0306: . AI will see only the tools from that server and won't be able to touch, e.g., options by accident.
strings.t0307: What can AI actually do through MCP?
strings.t0308: Each of our servers exposes its own tools list — you can see the full set right after connecting by asking the assistant: "What IQ Option tools do you have?". In total this is reading market data, portfolio and trades, plus methods to open/close positions. Which methods are available to a specific token is determined by its scope (read + optionally trade per market).
strings.t0309: How do I make sure AI can't trade?
strings.t0313: any of the four "Trade · …" checkboxes. Read stays on (it can't be turned off — AI needs it), and any attempt to call a trading tool will return
strings.t0314: . This is the safest setup for research use cases.
strings.t0315: Can I allow trading on only one instrument?
strings.t0317: — AI will be able to open/close crypto positions, but options, CFD and forex stay read-only. Useful when you're testing the AI setup on a market you're comfortable with.
strings.t0318: What if I want to drop a trade permission from an existing token?
strings.t0320: , find the token and uncheck the relevant box (e.g.
strings.t0321: ). The permission is removed instantly — the server will start returning
strings.t0322: on trading calls for that market. The token value itself doesn't change, so no need to touch your assistant's config.
strings.t0325: , find the compromised token and hit Revoke. All requests with it stop working instantly. Create a new one and update the assistant's config.
strings.t0326: What are the limits on tokens and servers?
strings.t0327: One account can have multiple active tokens at once (visible in the Active tokens list). One token works across all 4 servers. If you want separate keys per integration for easier revocation — create several and give them meaningful names.
strings.t0328: My assistant doesn't see the tools after setup. What now?
strings.t0329: Checklist: (1) restart the assistant completely, (2) make sure the token is copied without extra whitespace at the start/end, (3) check that the URL has no trailing slash and matches your subscription, (4) make sure the Authorization header starts exactly with
strings.t0330: (note the space), (5) check the assistant's logs — usually it's a JSON syntax error in the config.
strings.t0331: Which assistant is the easiest to set up from scratch?
strings.t0332: If you're not a developer — Claude Desktop or Cherry Studio: both have a UI that handles the config for you. If you live in code — Cursor or Claude Code will feel more natural.
strings.t0334: At the moment — yes, free for all IQ Option clients with an active account. You only pay for your AI assistant's subscription (Claude Pro / ChatGPT Plus, etc.).
strings.t0337: AI assistants are independent third-party tools. IQ Option does not control their behaviour and accepts no liability for decisions made on the basis of AI output. Trading CFDs / options / forex / crypto involves a high risk of capital loss. Every trade is taken at your own responsibility. Before going live — practise on a demo account.
strings.t0338: © 2026 IQ Option · Built on Model Context Protocol
